title: Fix std::randomize() in static function with static class members (#7167)
body:
## Summary
Fix `std::randomize()` called from a static class function with static class members in the `with` clause generating invalid C++ that fails to compile.

## Issue
Fixes verilator/verilator #7167

## Changes
- `V3Randomize.cpp` (`RandomizeVisitor`): Detect static context via `m_ftaskp->isStatic()`, mark generated helper function (`newRandomizeStdFunc`) and `stdrand` variable as static so V3Class moves them to `__Vclpkg`
- `V3Randomize.cpp` (`CaptureVisitor`): Add `m_staticContext` member; in `getVarRefCaptureMode()`, return `CAP_NO` instead of `CAP_THIS` for static class members when in static context
- `V3Randomize.cpp` (`CaptureVisitor` constructor): Accept `staticContext` parameter, propagated from `RandomizeVisitor`

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_randomize_std_static.{v,py}` — `std::randomize()` from a static function with a static queue member and `inside` constraint

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
